notes: deterministic calculus and coordinate geometry

question: Negative binomial questions Q4
topic: geometric binomial and negative binomial meter-reading probabilities
question_text: House meter reading success probability is 0.4. Find probability first reading is taken on or before 7th visit; exactly 3 readings in first 7 visits; 3rd reading on 7th visit; and 3rd reading on or before 7th visit.
mark_scheme_working: For exactly 3 successes in 7 visits, Y~B(7,0.4), P(Y=3)=C(7,3)(0.4)^3(0.6)^4≈0.2903
For the 3rd success on the 7th visit, use negative binomial: C(6,2)(0.4)^3(0.6)^4≈0.1244
For the 3rd success on or before the 7th visit, P(Y>=3) for Y~B(7,0.4)≈0.5801
unsupported_reason: model-switch explanation remains manual-only
notes: deterministic geometric/binomial/negative-binomial arithmetic

question: Negative binomial questions Q5
topic: negative binomial exact probability expectation and normal approximation for sample mean
question_text: Steve uploads 5 suitable videos per week; each filmed video is suitable with probability 0.25. Find probability he films 11 videos to get 5 suitable videos, expected number filmed, and over 40 weeks estimate P(mean weekly filmed videos >18).
mark_scheme_working: Let X~NB(5,0.25), total videos filmed to get 5 suitable videos
Var(X)=r(1-p)/p^2=60; by CLT, sample mean over 40 weeks is approximately N(20,60/40)
